# Evidence Brief - Education (SDG 4): Kenya

*Generated by CEDAR on 2026-06-20T14:52:41Z | Glass-box mode: every figure is cited below.*

## Key findings

- [+] Primary completion rate rose from 87.8 to 102.2 % of relevant age group between 2005 and 2016 (+16.4%, +1.4%/yr). [1]
- [OK] As of 2016, the value of 102.2 meets the benchmark of 100 (SDG 4.1: universal primary completion); target achieved. [2]

## Data confidence & caveats

- **Primary completion rate** - confidence: **Low** (coverage 33%, latest 2016).
    - Caveat: Sparse series: only 4 of 12 years carry a value (survey-dependent indicator). Treat year-on-year movement with caution.
    - Caveat: Latest available data point is 2016; figures may lag current conditions.

## Methods & reproducibility

All figures are pulled from authoritative APIs and computed deterministically (trend = first vs latest observation; CAGR = compound annual rate; gap = distance to published benchmark). No figure is model-generated. Re-run any number with the query URLs in the evidence ledger below.
